comments.text: Some comments on this problem: That $c(p,q)$ is nondecreasing in $q$ for fixed $p$ is a straightforward consequence of the definitions. The estimate $c(p,\binom{p-1}{2})\le1/2$ appears incorrect. If $k\ge3$, then we can show that $H(n;2k,\binom{2k-1}{2})\ge c_kn^{1-1/k}$ , so that $c(2k,\binom{2k-1}{2})\ge1-1/k>1/2$. Indeed, given an $n$-vertex graph $G$ in which every set of $2k$ vertices spans at least $\binom{2k-1}{2}$ edges, every set of $2k$ vertices of $\overline G$ spans at most $2k-1$ edges, so $\overline G$ is $C_{2k}$-free. Then $e(\overline G)\le b_kn^{1+1/k}$ by Bondy–Simonovits, so $\omega(G)=\alpha(\overline G)\ge n^2/(2e(\overline G)+n)\ge c_kn^{1-1/k}$. We do however have $c(p,\binom{p-1}{2})\le2p/(2p+1)$, since Erdős proved that if $h(k,l)$ denotes the least $N$ such that every $N$-vertex graph contains either a cycle of length $\le k$ or an independent set of size $l$, then $h(k,l)>l^{1+1/(2k)}$ for fixed $k$ and all sufficiently large $l$; taking $k=p$ and $l=\lceil n^{2p/(2p+1)}\rceil$ gives, for all large $n$, an $n$-vertex graph $F$ of girth $>p$ with $\alpha(F)<l$, so every $p$ vertices of $F$ span at most $p-1$ edges, and hence $\overline F$ has every $p$-set spanning at least $\binom{p-1}{2}$ edges while $\omega(\overline F)=\alpha(F)<\lceil n^{2p/(2p+1)}\rceil$. (https://www.renyi.hu/~p_erdos/1959-06.pdf) We also have $c(p,\binom{p-2}{2}+1)\ge1/2$ for $p\ge4$; this follows from showing that every $n$-vertex graph $F$ such that every set of $p$ vertices spans at most $2p-4$ edges has independence number at least $\sqrt{n/(p-2)}-1/(p-2)$ for $p\ge4$. And $c(4,2)=1/2$ by known estimates on $r(3,t)$; the $p=4$ question then reduces to asking if $n$-vertex $\{C_3,C_4\}$-free graphs always satisfy $\alpha\ge n^{1/2+\varepsilon}$ for some absolute $\varepsilon>0$, or if one can get $\alpha=n^{1/2+o(1)}$.
